Placing a `<WebView>` in a parent element that uses a `wrap_content layout_height` can lead to subtle bugs; use `match_parent` instead

location.message: `wrap_content` here may not work well with WebView below

issue.message: The bool "value_only_present_in_w320dp" in values-w320dp has no declaration in the base `values` folder; this can lead to crashes when the resource is queried in a configuration that does not match this qualifier

The layout "layout_320_smallest_width" in layout-sw320dp has no declaration in the base `layout` folder; this can lead to crashes when the resource is queried in a configuration that does not match this qualifier

The layout "layout_smallest_width" in layout-sw320dp has no declaration in the base `layout` folder; this can lead to crashes when the resource is queried in a configuration that does not match this qualifier

The layout "layout_smallest_width" in layout-sw720dp has no declaration in the base `layout` folder; this can lead to crashes when the resource is queried in a configuration that does not match this qualifier

A newer version of `compileSdk` than 30 is available: 37
Avoid using "`mm`" as units (it does not work accurately on all devices); use "`dp`" instead
Avoid using "`in`" as units (it does not work accurately on all devices); use "`dp`" instead
Should use "`sp`" instead of "`dp`" for text sizes
Should use "`sp`" instead of "`dp`" for text sizes
Should use "`sp`" instead of "`dp`" for text sizes

Should use "`sp`" instead of "`dp`" for text sizes

No orientation specified, and the default is horizontal. This is a common source of bugs when children are added dynamically.
Wrong orientation? No orientation specified, and the default is horizontal, yet this layout has multiple children where at least one has `layout_width="match_parent"`
Wrong orientation? No orientation specified, and the default is horizontal, yet this layout has multiple children where at least one has `layout_width="match_parent"`
Avoid using `"px"` as units; use `"dp"` instead
Avoid using `"px"` as units; use `"dp"` instead
Avoid using `"px"` as units; use `"dp"` instead

When you define `paddingLeft` you should probably also define `paddingRight` for right-to-left symmetry
Consider replacing `android:paddingLeft` with `android:paddingStart="6dip"` to better support right-to-left layouts
Consider replacing `android:drawableRight` with `android:drawableEnd="@drawable/an_other_image"` to better support right-to-left layouts
Consider replacing `android:drawableLeft` with `android:drawableStart="@drawable/fourth_image"` to better support right-to-left layouts
